name: Info
format:
About

Smart Door Devices is an IoT-based smart entry monitoring system designed to demonstrate connected door security and automation features through a simple web interface. The system combines doorbell notification, motion detection, lighting indication, and device communication into one dashboard for monitoring and interaction.

System Information

Gateway: Raspberry Pi with OpenThread Border Router

Wireless Communication: Thread / BLE

Data Handling: MQTT with Node-RED Dashboard

End Device: QPG6200L

Interface: Node-RED Dashboard 2.0

How It Works

The Smart Door Devices system operates through a combination of wireless communication, gateway processing, and a real-time dashboard interface.

  • The QPG6200L device collects sensor data such as motion detection, button presses (doorbell), and device status, then transmits this information over a low-power wireless network (Thread/BLE).
  • The gateway (Raspberry Pi with OpenThread Border Router) receives this data and acts as a bridge between the device network and the dashboard system.
  • The gateway forwards incoming data to Node-RED, where logic flows process the information, trigger events (such as alerts or lighting changes), and prepare it for visualization.
  • The Node-RED dashboard displays real-time device states and allows users to interact with the system through a web-based interface.
  • This setup enables seamless communication between hardware devices and the user interface, creating a responsive and interactive smart door monitoring system.

User Guide

This dashboard provides a simple interface for monitoring and interacting with smart door features in real time.

  • Use the dashboard to monitor system components, including the doorbell status, motion detection activity, and lighting system behavior.
  • Click the “Ring Doorbell” button to simulate a visitor event and observe how the system responds (notifications, UI updates, and sound alerts).
  • The motion detector section will automatically update when movement is detected, indicating activity near the door.
  • The lighting system panel shows whether the door light is currently on or off and reflects any automatic or manual changes.
  • Navigate between pages (e.g., Smart Devices and Project Info) using the sidebar while maintaining your session.
  • Use the GitHub repository link provided below to access the full project code, documentation, and implementation details.
